rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For Posting Of Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 6 of 7] As of the August 7, 2025 hearing on Petitioner's Motion to Set Trial, Movants were aware of their written objections to Petitioner's alleged right to file the TAP without leave made in the April Status Report, July Status Report, and Joint Opposition, and their perception that the court had admonished them at the May 5, 2025 hearing to not file a motion to strike. The tentative ruling granting the Motion to Set Trial on specific legal basis issued on August 5, 2025, and was adopted verbatim into the August 20, 2025 Order Granting Motion to Set Trial. These were all facts known as of the August 7, 2025 hearing, and as of the August 20, 2025. Facts Respondents were aware of at time of the original ruling are not "new or different." (See, e.g., Yolo County Dept. of Child Support Services v. Myers (2016) 248 Cal.App.4th 42; Shiffer v. CBS Corp. (2015) 240 Cal.App.4th 246; Gaines v. Fidelity National Title Ins. Co. (2013) 222 Cal.App.4th 25, judgment affirmed Gaines v. Fidelity National Title Ins. Co. (2106) 62 Cal.4th 1081.) As summarized in the Background section above, Respondents raised their concerns about the TAP to the court several times, including at the August 7, 2025 hearing on the motion to set trial. Respondents stated repeatedly, in filings and at hearings, beginning at the latest in April 2025, their objection to the TAP being filed without a separate motion and order authorizing further amendment, and their intention to file various motions in response to the TAP being filed, including a motion to strike. Respondents presented their objections and opposition to Petitioner's Motion to Set Trial, in both their filed opposition papers and at the August 7, 2025 hearing on that motion. The tentative ruling granting the motion to set trial was timely posted prior to the hearing. Respondents' counsel appeared at the August 7, 2025 hearing to challenge the tentative ruling. The Minutes of that hearing reflect the vigorous argument presented by Respondents' counsel in opposing the motion to set a trial date on the TAP, and in opposition to the TAP having been filed without prior leave of court. As of the August 5, 2025 tentative, ruling, the Augst 7, 2025 hearing on the Motion to Set Trial, and entry of the August 20, 2025 Order Granting Motion to Set Trial, the court, too, was aware of the Respondents' concerns, opposition, and objections to: (1) the TAP being filed without prior leave of court, (2) the court allowing it to have been filed, by oral order at the May 5, 2025 hearing, and (3) Petitioner's motion to set the TAP for trial in December 2025.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For Posting Of Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 7 of 7] The August 20, 2025 Order After Hearing summarizes and addresses the oral arguments made at the August 7, 2025 hearing regarding sua sponte bifurcation of issues, sending the prior second amended petition to trial, and the relation-back doctrine as applied to the TAP's amendments. In that context, the Order states at 4:9-14, "Respondents argue the TAP ought not to have been filed, and the new claims could have been filed in a separate, new petition. Respondents did not bring a motion to strike the TAP. At the May 5, 2025 status hearing, the court allowed the TAP to be filed without a motion for leave to amend. Respondents do not offer authority that requires Petitioner to have brought a separate petition pleading, rather than filing a further amended pleading." The August 20 Order makes no findings that the Respondents "should have raised their concerns regarding, or otherwise moved to strike [the TAP]," as the Motion argues it does. The Respondents had raised their concerns to the court, prior to and at the August 7, 2025 hearing on Motion to Set Trial, repeatedly and substantively, over the prior several months. There is no showing these were "new or different facts" discovered afterward and properly the subject of amotion for reconsideration under Code of Civil Procedure section 1008(a). Petitioner shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondents Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order After Hearing Granted
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 7] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Respondents' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order After Hearing Granting Motion ("Motion"). Per the Notice of Motion, Respondents Betty Bong and Annie Yi Tang, in her individual capacity, bring the Motion "pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1008, subdivision (a), on the grounds that "different facts, circumstances, or law" exist warranting reconsideration of this court's August 20, 2025 Order After Hearing Granting Motion to Set Trial." However, the Memorandum of Points and Authorities "ask[s] that the Court reconsider its Trial Motion Order, and its implementing Trial Setting Order, and thereafter exercise its inherent authority to reconsider its previous interim order allowing Petitioner's filing of the TAP at all." (MPA 9:25-27.) The Notice of Motion controls. (See, Code of Civil Proc. sec 1010; CRC, rule 3.1110). The Motion is DENIED, for the following reasons. PROCEDURAL BACKGROUND RELEVANT TO THIS MOTION Petitioner Mary Bong's initial Petition For Order Directing Conveyance Or Transfer Of Property To Probate Estate was filed January 8, 2021. A First Amended Petition was filed April 8, 2021. The initial Opposition/Objection was filed June 23, 2021. On August 24, 2023, over Respondents' opposition to the motion, the court entered Order Granting Petitioner Mary Bong's Motion To Amend First Amended Petition For Order Directing Conveyance Or Transfer Of Property To Probate Estate. The Second Amended Petition was subsequently filed August 28, 2023. Respondents filed responses and objections to the Second Amended Petition, in their individual and trustee capacities, on October 9, 2023. On April 11, 2025, Petitioner Mary Bong filed Third Amended Petition For Orders Directing Conveyance Or Transfer Of Property To Probate Estate, And To Set Aside Voidable Transactions. On May 2, 2025, she filed Corrected Third Amended Petition For Orders Directing Conveyance Or Transfer Of Property To Probate Estate; To Set Aside Voidable Transactions; To Suspend Administrator; To Suspend Officers And Directors Of JBE; And For An Accounting. Neither amended pleading was filed with prior leave of court. In advance of the May 5, 2025 hearing, the parties filed Joint Status Report on April 29, 2025. Relevant to this Motion for Reconsideration, and discussed in the moving papers, the Joint Status Report stated, Annie Tang and Betty Bong, in their individual capacities, Betty, in her capacity as Co-Trustee, and JBE each believe that filing of the TAP requires leave of Court, and intend to file a Motion to Strike the TAP and for sanctions for the late and improper filing of the TAP. Annie Tang, as Administrator of the Estate (the "Administrator"), objects to the TAP as defective for two reasons: (1) an amended pleading requires the same notice of hearing as the pleading it amends under California Rules of Court Rule 7.53, and no notice of hearing was served, and (2) under California Rules of Court Rule 7.102, the title of each pleading must clearly and completely identify the nature of the relief sought or granted, and the TAP fails to identify the new relief sought. In fact, no hearing date has been set for the TAP. For these reasons, the Administrator contends the TAP should be stricken. JBE also intends to file a motion to expunge the Notices of Pending Action recorded against the subject properties and for fees and costs in connection therewith. Mary believes that the TAP (and Notices) are proper; however, if the Court indicates otherwise at the May 5, 2024 Status Hearing, then Mary will promptly file a motion for leave. (April 29, 2025 Joint Status Report at 2:11-25.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For Posting Of Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 7] A further hearing on the Case Management Conference and Petitioner's pleading was held July 7, 2025. Per the Minutes of the hearing, Discussion was held regarding the third amended petition filed to add more assets, to include seven properties in the real estate holding firm James Bong Enterprise, Inc., that discovery has been ongoing but there has not been any discovery on the new claims, and that Mr. Cross is requesting to file a motion regarding the 5-year statute. The Court authorizes counsel to file a Motion Tolling the 5-year Statute of Limitations set for August 7, 2025, to 2:00 p.m. in Dept. 204. After hearing, all matters were continued to September 10, 2025; a joint status statement was due one week before the continued hearing date. Subsequently, the Case management Conference was taken off calendar and hearing on the Petition was continued to December 15, 2025. On July 16, 2025, Petitioner filed Motion to Set Trial, for hearing on August 7, 2015. On July 25, 2025, Respondents filed opposition to the motion. On August 5, 2025, a tentative ruling granting the Motion to Set Trial was issued. Respondents timely gave notice of their intent to appear and contest the tentative ruling. On August 7, 2025, hearing was held on Petitioner's Motion to Set Trial. Per the Minutes of the hearing, Ms. Gallagher and Mr. Rodriguez [on behalf of Respondents, in their individual and trustee capacities] challenged the court's tentative decision to grant Mary Bong's Motion to Set Trial. Ms. Gallagher and Mr. Rodriguez both argued that the trial should proceed only on the Second Amended Petition, not the Third, due to concerns over newly raised claims, inadequate time for discovery, and potential prejudice to respondents. Counsel emphasized that the Third Amended Petition significantly expanded the scope of the litigation with new claims and parties. They argued that these new claims were pled only in April 2025, giving respondents insufficient time to prepare for trial set in December 2025. Respondents urged the Court to bifurcate the issues, allowing trial to proceed on the Second Amended Petition while requiring petitioner to file a separate petition for the newly added claims, thereby avoiding prejudice and preserving respondents' right to pretrial motions and discovery. Mr. Cross opposed bifurcation, arguing that all of the petitions arise from the same set of facts and that judicial efficiency would be undermined by separate trials. Mr. Cross contended that respondents had long been on notice for the claims now pled in the Third Amended Petition and had been offered stipulations to extend the five-year period, which they refused. He further stated that many of the factual issues were central to both the Second and Third Amended Petition and could not practically be severed. The court took the motion under submission. On August 20, 2025, Order After Hearing Granting Motion to Set Trial was entered. On August 22, 2024 Order Setting Trial in the Department of the Presiding Judge was entered.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For Posting Of Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 7] On May 5, 2025, hearing on Case Management Conference and Petitioner's pleading was held concurrently. Per the Minutes of the May 5, 2025 hearing, Discussion was held regarding the newly amended petition alleging that the decedent signatures on certain documents were for [sic] forged by Annie, Betty and Attorney Pung, that Court did receive the joint status report filed on April 29, 2025, and that the court does permit filing of third amended petition. Mr. Cross [appearing on behalf of Petitioner] stated that he has depositions scheduled in early June and will need a 60 day continuance. Ms. Grossman [appearing on behalf of trustees] said she is ready for trial. The court states that it would like to have all petitions tried together." After hearing, all matters were continued to July 7, 2025, and an amended status report was due one week before the continued hearing date. In advance of the July 7, 2025 hearing, the parties filed Joint Status Report on July 2, 2025. Relevant to this Motion for Reconsideration, and referenced in the moving papers, the parties stated, [Under heading "Pleading Status"] Mary Bong ("Mary") filed her Corrected Third Amended Petition ("TAP") on May 2, 2025. The TAP is not yet at issue, as responsive pleadings are not due until July 7, 2025. Annie Tang ("Annie") and Betty Bong ("Betty") believe that some of the new claims and causes of action in raised in the TAP are improper due to lack of standing, statute of limitations issues and/or lack of jurisdiction. Accordingly, after filing and serving their responses to the TAP, Tang and/or Betty plan to challenge the offending claims and causes of action either by motion for judgment on the pleadings, motion to strike and/or motion for summary adjudication as appropriate. [ ] [Under heading "Trial Setting"] While has Betty raised the possibility of having her petition to construe the Revocable Trust to include a will-like provision bifurcated and tried first, the Court indicated at the May 5, 2025 Status Conference that it is not inclined to bifurcate the matter. To the extent that is still the Court's inclination, all related petitions would need to be set for trial together. Mary believes that the Court should set trial of all petitions and issues in late October or November 2025, to avoid problems with the five-year statute. Tang and Betty do not believe it is appropriate to set the TAP for trial in late-October or November 2025. Because Mary chose not to file the TAP until May 2, 2025, it will not be at issue until July 7, 2025. The TAP raises entirely new claims and causes of action, with respect to which Tang and Betty will need to conduct written discovery. Likewise, Tang and Betty plan to challenge some of the newly raised claims and causes of action either by motion for judgment on the pleadings, motion to strike and/or motion for summary adjudication as appropriate. Tang and Betty should not be deprived of their rights to proper discovery and to challenge the TAP solely because Mary chose to wait until eight months prior to expiration of the 5-year rule to file her TAP. (July 2, 2025 Joint Status Statement at 2:3-10 and 3:17-4:5.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For Posting Of Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 5 of 7] Regardless, the court is willing to deem the Motion filed as of September 4, 2025, and rule on the merits. A substantive opposition - albeit to the unfiled motion papers served on September 4, 2025 - was filed on September 12, 2025. There does not appear to be prejudice to Petitioner in allowing the untimely Motion to proceed to hearing. The Motion Does Not Meet the Statutory Requirements Waiving the procedural defect does not save the Motion, however. The Motion also fails to meet the substantive requirements of the statute. Per the Notice of Motion, the August 20, 2025 Order "stands on a presumption that Respondents should have raised their concerns regarding, or otherwise moved to strike, Petitioner Mary Bong's ("Petitioner's") Corrected Third Amended Petition for Orders Directing Conveyance or Transfer of Property to Probate Estate, and to Set Aside Voidable Transactions (the "TAP") prior to opposing Petitioner's Motion to Set Trial on the claims contained in the TAP. However, the Trial Motion Order fails to acknowledge Respondents' pleadings and the Court's specific instructions that Respondents shall not to file a Motion to Strike or a Demurrer on the TAP, as stated during the May 5, 2025 hearing in this matter. [emphasis in original] The MPA at 9:19-24 concludes Respondents' argument: "That the Responding Parties informed the Court of their intentions to move to strike the TAP, and that the Court instructed them not to do so while also accepting the TAP without requiring Petitioner to file a motion for leave to amend, constitute "different facts" than those relied upon by the Court in making its Trial Motion Order, and thus support this Motion for Reconsideration. (See Code of Civ. Proc., at [sec.] 1008(a).)" The argument for reconsideration appears to be that, because the August 20, 2025 Order did not include "mention of 1) Responding Parties' written objections to Petitioner's alleged right to file the TAP without leave (see: April Status Report, July Status Report, Joint Opposition); or 2) the Court's clear admonition at the May 5, 2025 hearing that Responding Parties shall not file a Motion or Strike or Demurrer in response to the TAP" (MPA at 7:3-13), those are the "different facts" satisfying the statutory requirements of CCP 1008(a), and warranting reconsideration of the August 20 Order. Movants appear to be confused as to the necessary showing on a motion brought under CCP 1008(a). A motion for reconsideration must be based on "new or different facts, circumstances, or law." A motion under Code of Civil Procedure section 1008(a) must state by affidavit what new or different facts, circumstances, or law has been shown, warranting reconsideration and modification, amendment or revocation. The Motion here does not present new or different facts, circumstances, or law. Although two declarations are submitted in support of the Motion, with a transcript of the July 7, 2025 hearing included as an exhibit to the Declaration of Kevin M. Rodriguez, neither states what "what new or different facts, circumstances, or law" are claimed to be shown warranting reconsideration of the August 20, 2025 Order. Neither states a satisfactory explanation for the failure to produce the purported "new or different" evidence at an earlier time, prior to issuance of the August 20 Order After Hearing.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For Posting Of Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 4 of 7] DISCUSSION Through considering and ruling on this Motion, the court does not endorse or accept Movants' characterization of its statements or comments made at the May 5, 2025 hearing. Judge Moody has no personal memory of directing, instructing, or prohibiting Respondents from filing a motion or other pleading in response to the TAP, at the May 5, 2025 hearing or at any other time. The Minutes of that hearing do not reflect such a statement. There is no transcript of the hearing before the court. Further, such instruction would significantly deviate from Judge Moody's practice as a judicial officer for over ten years. As discussed below, consideration, analysis, and determination of this Motion brought pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1008(a) is not effected by Respondents' decision to not bring any of the motions they discussed in their filings between April and July to challenge the validity of the TAP, whatever the reason or reasons for that decision. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 1008(a), "When an application for an order has been made to a judge, or to a court, and [granted], any party affected by the order may, within 10 days after service upon the party of written notice of entry of the order and based upon new or different facts, circumstances, or law, make application to the same judge or court that made the order, to reconsider the matter and modify, amend, or revoke the prior order. The party making the application shall state by affidavit what application was made before, when and to what judge, what order or decisions were made, and what new or different facts, circumstances, or law are claimed to be shown." The Motion is Untimely A motion under Code of Civil Procedure section 1008(a) must be filed within 10 days after service of notice of entry of the subject order, and must state by affidavit what new or different facts, circumstances, or law has been shown, warranting reconsideration and modification, amendment or revocation. The Motion was filed untimely. Movant asks the court to deem the Motion filed within the statutory time period anyway. The Order After Hearing Granting Motion To Set Trial was entered and served by the court, via mail, on all parties' attorneys of record, on August 20, 2025. Although the moving papers were initially submitted for filing on September 4, 2025, all of those papers were rejected from filing because the moving party had not complied with Local Rue 14.10, and had not obtained an approved hearing date prior to submitting the motion for filing. An approved hearing date was later obtained and the moving papers were resubmitted and filed on September 12, 2025, more than 10 days after service of notice of entry of the Order. (See, Petak Decl. pars 10-16.) Ms. Petak's declaration at paragraph 16 states a court clerk informed her on September 8, 2025 "that Respondents could include a request that the Reconsideration Motion be deemed filed as September 4, 2025 within the re-filed Reconsideration Motion itself." The final section of the Motion's Memorandum of Points and Authorities argues that the Motion should be deemed filed as of September 4, 2025 because it submitted the moving papers for filing and served the relevant parties on that date. The papers state "Respondents were unable to confirm a hearing date for the Motion prior to filing," and Movant was "unable to connect with the Probate Department courtroom" and "Respondents' inability to secure a hearing date prior to filing" in compliance with Local Rule 14.10 (MPA at 7:18, 11:5-7, 11:9; Petak Decl. par. 10-16) without elaborating on why they were unable to secure an approved hearing date from the court clerk prior to submitting their moving papers on September 4, 2025.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE BETTY DUGAN FAMILY TRUST AS RESTATED ON APRIL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondents Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions In The Amount Of $5,115.00 Against Peti-Tioner Sharon Wilson And The Law Office Of Mark L. Kelly, And Mark L. Kelly
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Respondent Jodi Birenbaum's Motion for Sanctions in the Amount of $5,115.00 Against Petitioner Sharon Wilson and the Law Office of Mark L. Kelly, and Mark L. Kelly. Per the Notice of Motion, the request for sanctions is brought pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sec.128.5, on the grounds that Petition for Accounting, Demand for Distribution, for Breaches of Trust, Removal and Appointment of Independent Trustee ("Petition") was filed by attorney Mark L. Kelly on behalf of Petitioner Sharon Wilson in the wrong venue, by a person who lacks standing to bring the Petition. The 21-day "safe harbor" requirements of Code of Civil Procedure sec.128.5 were observed. A Notice of Non-Opposition was filed October 8, 2025. The Motion for Sanctions is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. The tentative ruling on the Ocotber 10, 2025 demurrer and motion in this matter, sustaining with leave to amend Respondent's demurrer to the Petition on grounds of lack of standing and improper venue and denying without prejudice the request to transfer venue, was not challenged by either party. The court intends to adopt that unchallenged tentative ruling as an order. In the unchallenged tentative ruling, Petitioner was allowed 10 days from entry of that Order in which to file an amended pleading addressing the grounds for demurrer. That 10-day period has not yet expired. Petitioner shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
